data.content:
### CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: Executing Custom Plugin Scripts

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

This snippet illustrates how to execute custom plugin scripts, such as a linter, using the '${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}' environment variable. By referencing scripts like 'bin/lint.js' relative to the plugin's root, operations become self-contained and portable, allowing the plugin to run its own tools effectively.

```markdown
--- description: Run custom linter from plugin allowed-tools: Bash(node:*) ---

Lint results: !`node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/lint.js $1`

Review the linting output and suggest fixes.
```

--------------------------------

### Organizing Namespaced Plugin Commands in Subdirectories

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

This example demonstrates how to organize plugin commands into subdirectories to create namespaces, such as 'review/' or 'deploy/'. This practice helps group related commands, improves clarity in the '/help' output (e.g., '(plugin:plugin-name:namespace)'), and is recommended for plugins with five or more commands.

```filesystem
plugin-name/
└── commands/
    ├── review/
    │   ├── security.md    # /security (plugin:plugin-name:review)
    │   └── style.md       # /style (plugin:plugin-name:review)
    └── deploy/
        ├── staging.md     # /staging (plugin:plugin-name:deploy)
        └── prod.md        # /prod (plugin:plugin-name:deploy)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Nested Command Paths with Custom Discovery

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-structure/references/component-patterns.md

JSON manifest configuration for hierarchical command organization using custom paths. Claude Code requires explicit path configuration for nested command discovery since automatic nested discovery is not supported.

```json
{
  "commands": [
    "./commands/ci",
    "./commands/deployment",
    "./commands/management"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Test Commands - Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

Basic bash commands to manually test functionality and verify output. These commands display the current date and echo a test string to validate command execution and output.

```bash
date
echo "test"
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Command Output in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Demonstrates how to build a project and subsequently validate its output. This command executes a build script and then checks the exit code, the existence of a 'dist' directory, and the count of files within it using Bash commands.

```markdown
---
description: Build and validate output
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---

Build: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/build.sh`

Validate output:
- Exit code: !`echo $?`
- Output exists: !`test -d dist && echo "✓" || echo "✗"`
- File count: !`find dist -type f | wc -l`

Report build status and any validation failures.
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Data Management with Cache and Bash Commands

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Demonstrates a command pattern for managing plugin-specific persistent data, such as analysis results. It shows how to create a cache directory and write a timestamp file using Bash commands. This pattern requires `Bash(*)`, `Read`, and `Write` tools for full functionality.

```markdown
---
description: Save analysis results to plugin cache
allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read, Write
---

Cache directory: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/cache/

Analyze @$1 and save results to cache:
!`mkdir -p ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/cache && date > ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/cache/last-run.txt`

Store analysis for future reference and comparison.
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Command Inputs in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Demonstrates input validation within a Claude command, ensuring that the provided environment argument is one of the allowed values ('dev', 'staging', 'prod'). It uses both a Bash `grep -E` command for programmatic validation and an inline `$IF` condition for conditional logic.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy to environment with validation
argument-hint: [environment]
---

Validate environment: !`echo "$1" | grep -E "^(dev|staging|prod)$" || echo "INVALID"`

$IF($1 in [dev, staging, prod],
  Deploy to $1 environment using validated configuration,
  ERROR: Invalid environment '$1'. Must be one of: dev, staging, prod
)
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Test Claude Command Invocation and Debugging (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This set of commands outlines a manual testing procedure for Claude commands, focusing on execution and debugging. It instructs on starting Claude Code in debug mode, verifying command visibility with /help, invoking the command with and without arguments, and inspecting the debug logs for errors or unexpected behavior. This allows for direct observation of command interaction.

```bash
# 1. Start Claude Code
claude --debug

# 2. Check command appears in help
> /help
# Look for your command in the list

# 3. Invoke command without arguments
> /my-command
# Check for reasonable error or behavior

# 4. Invoke with valid arguments
> /my-command arg1 arg2
# Verify expected behavior

# 5. Check debug logs
tail -f ~/.claude/debug-logs/latest
# Look for errors or warnings
```

--------------------------------

### Create Claude Code Markdown Command with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This snippet illustrates how to add configuration metadata to a Claude Code command using YAML frontmatter. It includes fields like `description`, `allowed-tools`, and `model` to customize command behavior and visibility. The frontmatter block must be at the very top of the Markdown file.

```markdown
---
description: Review code for security issues
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Bash(git:*)
model: sonnet
---

Review this code for security vulnerabilities...
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Basic Command Hook for Tool Pre-use Validation (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/hook-development/references/migration.md

This JSON configuration defines a basic command hook to be executed before a tool is used. It specifies a 'Bash' matcher, indicating that this hook applies to Bash commands, and runs an external shell script for validation, allowing for custom logic before tool execution.

```json
{
  "PreToolUse": [
    {
      "matcher": "Bash",
      "hooks": [
        {
          "type": "command",
          "command": "bash validate-bash.sh"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Organize Claude Code Command Files

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This snippet shows the recommended directory structure for storing Claude Code commands. Commands are organized into `.claude/commands/` and named with `.md` extensions for easy discovery and invocation. This structure applies to project, personal, and plugin commands.

```plaintext
.claude/commands/
├── review.md           # /review command
├── test.md             # /test command
└── deploy.md             # /deploy command
```

--------------------------------

### Environment-Aware Deployment Commands for Claude Plugins

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Describes a command pattern that adapts its behavior based on the target environment (e.g., `dev`, `staging`, `prod`). It loads environment-specific configuration from `config/$1.json` and confirms the deployment target using a Bash command. This pattern requires `Read` and `Bash(*)` tools.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy based on environment
argument-hint: [dev|staging|prod]
---

Environment config: @${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/$1.json

Environment check: !`echo "Deploying to: $1"`

Deploy application using $1 environment configuration.
Verify deployment and run smoke tests.
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Very Fast Command Checks with Command Hook (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/hook-development/references/migration.md

This Bash script shows a command hook performing very fast, simple checks using regular expressions. It extracts the command from tool input and approves it immediately if it matches a predefined list of safe commands ('ls', 'pwd', 'echo'). This is optimized for scenarios requiring minimal latency and straightforward logic.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Quick regex check
command=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if [[ "$command" =~ ^(ls|pwd|echo)$ ]]; then
  exit 0  # Safe commands
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Coordinate with Plugin Hooks in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Illustrates how Claude commands can be designed to interact with plugin hooks. This command stages and commits changes using Bash, which is then expected to trigger a pre-commit hook defined within the plugin for validation.

```markdown
---
description: Commit with pre-commit validation
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*)
---

Stage changes: !`git add $1`

Commit changes: !`git commit -m "$2"`

Note: This commit will trigger the plugin's pre-commit hook for validation.
Review hook output for any issues.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Commands Field in Plugin Manifest

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-structure/references/manifest-reference.md

Define additional directories or files containing command definitions in the plugin manifest. The commands field accepts either a single path string or an array of multiple paths, and supplements the default commands/ directory without replacing it.

```json
{
  "commands": "./custom-commands"
}
```

```json
{
  "commands": [
    "./commands",
    "./admin-commands",
    "./experimental-commands"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Implement a Basic Interactive Command for Plugin Setup

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/interactive-commands.md

This Markdown snippet outlines a basic interactive command for plugin setup using the `AskUserQuestion` and `Write` tools. It details a multi-step process for gathering configuration (deployment target, environment strategy, features) via interactive questions. The command then processes these answers to generate a YAML configuration file, providing a structured approach to interactive setup.

```markdown
description: Interactive setup command
allowed-tools: AskUserQuestion, Write
---

# Interactive Plugin Setup

This command will guide you through configuring the plugin with a series of questions.

## Step 1: Gather Configuration

Use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask:

**Question 1 - Deployment target:**
- header: "Deploy to"
- question: "Which deployment platform will you use?"
- options:
  - AWS (Amazon Web Services with ECS/EKS)
  - GCP (Google Cloud with GKE)
  - Azure (Microsoft Azure with AKS)
  - Local (Docker on local machine)

**Question 2 - Environment strategy:**
- header: "Environments"
- question: "How many environments do you need?"
- options:
  - Single (Just production)
  - Standard (Dev, Staging, Production)
  - Complete (Dev, QA, Staging, Production)

**Question 3 - Features to enable:**
- header: "Features"
- question: "Which features do you want to enable?"
- multiSelect: true
- options:
  - Auto-scaling (Automatic resource scaling)
  - Monitoring (Health checks and metrics)
  - CI/CD (Automated deployment pipeline)
  - Backups (Automated database backups)

## Step 2: Process Answers

Based on the answers received from AskUserQuestion:

1. Parse the deployment target choice
2. Set up environment-specific configuration
3. Enable selected features
4. Generate configuration files

## Step 3: Generate Configuration

Create `.claude/plugin-name.local.md` with:

```yaml
deployment_target: [answer from Q1]
environments: [answer from Q2]
features:
  auto_scaling: [true if selected in Q3]
  monitoring: [true if selected in Q3]
  ci_cd: [true if selected in Q3]
  backups: [true if selected in Q3]
---

# Plugin Configuration

Generated: [timestamp]
Target: [deployment_target]
Environments: [environments]
```

## Step 4: Confirm and Next Steps

Confirm configuration created and guide user on next steps.
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Environment-Aware Command Logic for Checks (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

This command adapts its behavior based on the specified environment, executing different validation checks for production versus non-production environments. It loads environment-specific configurations and conditionally runs full or basic test suites, security scans, and linting. This ensures appropriate testing and compliance based on the deployment target.

```Markdown
---
description: Run environment-appropriate checks
argument-hint: [environment]
allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read
---

Environment: $1

Load environment configuration: @${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/$1-checks.json

Determine check level: !`echo "$1" | grep -E "^prod$" && echo "FULL" || echo "BASIC"`

**For production environment:**
- Full test suite: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/test-full.sh`
- Security scan: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/security-scan.sh`
- Performance audit: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/perf-check.sh`
- Compliance check: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/compliance.sh`

**For non-production environments:**
- Basic tests: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/test-basic.sh`
- Quick lint: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/lint.sh`

Analyze results based on environment requirements:

**Production:** All checks must pass with zero critical issues
**Staging:** No critical issues, warnings acceptable
**Development:** Focus on blocking issues only

Report status and recommend proceed/block decision.
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Script for Automated Claude Command Test Suite

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This comprehensive Bash script (test-commands.sh) automates the testing of Claude command definition files (.md files) located in a specified directory. It iterates through each command, validates its structure and frontmatter using external helper scripts, and reports any failures.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# test-commands.sh - Command test suite

TEST_DIR=".claude/commands"
FAILED_TESTS=0

echo "Command Test Suite"
echo "=================="
echo

for cmd_file in "$TEST_DIR"/*.md; do
  cmd_name=$(basename "$cmd_file" .md)
  echo "Testing: $cmd_name"

  # Validate structure
  if ./validate-command.sh "$cmd_file"; then
    echo "  ✓ Structure valid"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Structure invalid"
    ((FAILED_TESTS++))
  fi

  # Validate frontmatter
  if ./validate-frontmatter.sh "$cmd_file"; then
    echo "  ✓ Frontmatter valid"
  else
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Script to Define Claude Command for Bash Execution Test

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This Bash script demonstrates how to create a Claude command definition file (.claude/commands/test-bash.md) that incorporates allowed-tools for Bash and utilizes backtick execution ('!') to embed outputs of 'date' and 'echo' commands directly into the Claude prompt.

```bash
# Create test command with bash execution
cat > .claude/commands/test-bash.md << 'EOF'
---
description: Test bash execution
allowed-tools: Bash(echo:*), Bash(date:*)
---

Current date: !`date`
Test output: !`echo "Hello from bash"`

Analysis of output above...
EOF
```

--------------------------------

### Configure stdio MCP Server - With Environment Variables

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/server-types.md

Configure a stdio MCP server with custom command path, arguments, and environment variables. Supports variable substitution using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} and environment variable references like ${MY_API_KEY}.

```json
{
  "my-server": {
    "command": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/servers/custom-server",
    "args": ["--config", "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config.json"],
    "env": {
      "API_KEY": "${MY_API_KEY}",
      "LOG_LEVEL": "debug",
      "DATABASE_URL": "${DB_URL}"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Markdown Examples for Claude Bash Command Edge Cases

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This Markdown code snippet demonstrates various Bash commands, enclosed in backticks, designed to test the Claude command runner's handling of execution failures (exit codes, non-existent commands) and special outputs (empty strings, large outputs). It's crucial for verifying command robustness.

```markdown
# Commands that might fail
!`exit 1`
!`false`
!`command-that-does-not-exist`

# Commands with special output
!`echo ""`
!`cat /dev/null`
!`yes | head -n 1000000`
```

--------------------------------

### Process Command with Plugin Settings Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/SKILL.md

Markdown documentation pattern for a command that reads plugin settings from `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`, parses the YAML frontmatter, and applies configuration to customize command behavior.

```markdown
---
description: Process data with plugin
allowed-tools: ["Read", "Bash"]
---

# Process Command

Steps:
1. Check if settings exist at `.claude/my-plugin.local.md`
2. Read configuration using Read tool
3. Parse YAML frontmatter to extract settings
4. Apply settings to processing logic
5. Execute with configured behavior
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Command: Deploy to a Specified Environment

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/simple-commands.md

This command defines a 'deploy' action for Claude to deploy a specific version to a target environment. It takes environment and version as arguments, performs pre-deployment checks, outlines deployment steps using 'kubectl' via 'Bash', and requires a rollback plan.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy to specified environment
argument-hint: [environment] [version]
allowed-tools: Bash(kubectl:*), Read
---

Deploy to $1 environment using version $2

**Pre-deployment Checks:**
1. Verify $1 configuration exists
2. Check version $2 is valid
3. Verify cluster accessibility: !`kubectl cluster-info`

**Deployment Steps:**
1. Update deployment manifest with version $2
2. Apply configuration to $1
3. Monitor rollout status
4. Verify pod health
5. Run smoke tests

**Rollback Plan:**
Document current version for rollback if issues occur.

Proceed with deployment? (yes/no)
```

```bash
> /deploy staging v1.2.3
```

--------------------------------

### Configuration-Based Deployment Command for Claude Plugins

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Presents a command pattern for deploying applications using plugin-specific configuration. It loads settings from `deploy-config.json` and integrates dynamic information like the current Git branch and application version using embedded Bash commands. This pattern requires `Read` and `Bash(*)` tools.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy using plugin settings
allowed-tools: Read, Bash(*)
---

Load configuration: @${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/deploy-config.json

Deploy to $1 environment using:
1. Configuration settings above
2. Current git branch: !`git branch --show-current`
3. Application version: !`cat package.json | grep version`

Execute deployment and monitor progress.
```

--------------------------------

### Shell - Smart Configuration Defaults

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Implements configuration with smart defaults using parameter expansion syntax. Allows overriding format, output, and verbosity through environment variables or command arguments, with configuration stored in .claude/plugin-name.local.md.

```shell
# Configuration:
- Format: ${FORMAT:-json}  # Defaults to json
- Output: ${OUTPUT:-stdout}  # Defaults to stdout
- Verbose: ${VERBOSE:-false}  # Defaults to false

Override with arguments:
  /command --format yaml --output file.txt --verbose

Or set in .claude/plugin-name.local.md:
---
format: yaml
output: custom.txt
verbose: true
---
```

--------------------------------

### Command with Embedded Usage Examples - Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/documentation-patterns.md

Demonstrates how to embed comprehensive examples within command documentation, showing basic usage, advanced options, and multiple use cases with expected inputs and outputs. This approach helps users understand command capabilities through concrete examples.

```bash
echo "This command performs feature analysis with multiple options."
echo ""
echo "## Basic Usage"
echo "/feature analyze src/"
echo "Analyzes all files in src/ directory for feature usage."
echo ""
echo "## Advanced Usage"
echo "/feature analyze src/ --detailed"
echo "Provides detailed analysis including:"
echo "- Feature breakdown by file"
echo "- Usage patterns"
echo "- Optimization suggestions"
echo ""
echo "## Use Cases"
echo ""
echo "Use Case 1: Quick overview"
echo "/feature analyze ."
echo "Get high-level feature summary of entire project."
echo ""
echo "Use Case 2: Specific directory"
echo "/feature analyze src/components"
echo "Focus analysis on components directory only."
echo ""
echo "Use Case 3: Comparison"
echo "/feature analyze src/ --compare baseline.json"
echo "Compare current features against baseline."
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Command Auto-Discovery Directory Structure

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

This snippet illustrates the directory structure Claude Code uses for automatic command discovery within plugins. Commands defined in the 'commands/' directory are identified at plugin load time without manual registration, appearing in the '/help' command with their respective plugin labels.

```filesystem
plugin-name/
├── commands/              # Auto-discovered commands
│   ├── foo.md            # /foo (plugin:plugin-name)
│   └── bar.md            # /bar (plugin:plugin-name)
└── plugin.json           # Plugin manifest
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Claude Command File Syntax Manually (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This snippet provides a set of manual Bash commands for immediate validation of a Claude command's file structure. It checks for correct YAML frontmatter, its closing marker, verifies the .md file extension, and confirms the file's presence in the expected directory. This helps ensure basic file integrity before automated checks.

```bash
# Validate YAML frontmatter
head -n 20 .claude/commands/my-command.md | grep -A 10 "^---"

# Check for closing frontmatter marker
head -n 20 .claude/commands/my-command.md | grep -c "^---" # Should be 2

# Verify file has .md extension
ls .claude/commands/*.md

# Check file is in correct location
test -f .claude/commands/my-command.md && echo "Found" || echo "Missing"
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Commands for Debugging Failed Claude Command Tests

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This section provides a series of Bash commands to diagnose common issues when Claude commands fail. It covers troubleshooting for commands not appearing in `/help`, argument substitution problems, and Bash command execution failures, including checks for file permissions, syntax, and `allowed-tools` configuration.

```bash
# Check file location
ls -la .claude/commands/my-command.md

# Check permissions
chmod 644 .claude/commands/my-command.md

# Check syntax
head -n 20 .claude/commands/my-command.md

# Restart Claude Code
claude --debug
```

```bash
# Verify syntax
grep '\$1' .claude/commands/my-command.md
grep '\$ARGUMENTS' .claude/commands/my-command.md

# Test with simple command first
echo "Test: \$1 and \$2" > .claude/commands/test-args.md
```

```bash
# Check allowed-tools
grep "allowed-tools" .claude/commands/my-command.md

# Verify command syntax
grep '!`' .claude/commands/my-command.md
```

--------------------------------

### Validate User Input Parameters

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

Illustrates the importance of validating command parameters before using them in operations like deployments. Input validation prevents invalid values from being processed and helps catch configuration errors early.

```markdown
# Risky - no validation
Deploy to $1 environment

# Better - with validation
Validate: !`echo "$1" | grep -E "^(dev|staging|prod)$" || echo "INVALID"`
Deploy to $1 environment (if valid)
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Specific Bash Command Output in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example shows how to enable a command to execute and retrieve output from specific Bash commands, like `git status:*` and `git diff:*`. This fine-grained control allows for specific shell interactions while maintaining security by limiting the scope of executable Bash commands. It's useful for commands needing to report on specific system states.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Invoke Plugin Skills in Claude Commands (Markdown)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Demonstrates how to reference plugin skills within Claude commands to leverage specialized knowledge. This example shows an API documentation command that uses the `api-docs-standards` skill to ensure consistency and completeness in the generated documentation.

```markdown
---
description: API documentation with best practices
argument-hint: [api-file]
---

Document the API in @$1 following our API documentation standards.

Use the api-docs-standards skill to ensure documentation includes:
- Endpoint descriptions
- Parameter specifications
- Response formats
- Error codes
- Usage examples

Note: This leverages the plugin's api-docs-standards skill for consistency.
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Input and Resources for Claude Plugin Build Command (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

This command defines a build process for specific environments with robust validation. It verifies the environment argument against allowed values, checks for the existence of a build script, and ensures the corresponding configuration file is available before proceeding. This approach provides comprehensive error handling and clear feedback on validation failures.

```Markdown
---
description: Build for specific environment with validation
argument-hint: [environment]
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---

Validate environment argument: !`echo "$1" | grep -E "^(dev|staging|prod)$" && echo "VALID" || echo "INVALID"`

Check build script exists: !`test -x ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/build.sh && echo "EXISTS" || echo "MISSING"`

Verify configuration available: !`test -f ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/$1.json && echo "FOUND" || echo "NOT_FOUND"`

If all validations pass:

**Configuration:** @${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/$1.json

**Execute build:** !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/build.sh $1 2>&1`

**Validation results:** !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/validate-build.sh $1 2>&1`

Report build status and any issues.

If validations fail:
- Explain which validation failed
- Provide expected values/locations
- Suggest corrective actions
- Document troubleshooting steps
```

### Validate Required Arguments in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Shows how to ensure that all necessary arguments are provided to a Claude command. This example validates both an environment and a version argument using a Bash `test -n` command and an `$IF` condition to guide the user if arguments are missing.

```markdown
---
description: Create deployment with version
argument-hint: [environment] [version]
---

Validate inputs: !`test -n "$1" -a -n "$2" && echo "OK" || echo "MISSING"`

$IF($1 AND $2,
  Deploy version $2 to $1 environment,
  ERROR: Both environment and version required. Usage: /deploy [env] [version]
)
```

### Claude Command: Run Tests for a Specific File

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/simple-commands.md

This command defines a 'test-file' action that allows Claude to run tests on a specified file. It takes a file path as an argument, uses 'npm test' or 'jest' via 'Bash', and instructs Claude to analyze the test results, including coverage and performance, and suggest fixes for failures.

```markdown
---
description: Run tests for specific file
argument-hint: [test-file]
allowed-tools: Bash(npm:*), Bash(jest:*)
---

Run tests for $1:

Test execution: !`npm test $1`

Analyze results:
- Tests passed/failed
- Code coverage
- Performance issues
- Flaky tests

If failures found, suggest fixes based on error messages.
```

```bash
> /test-file src/utils/helpers.test.ts
```

### Bash Script for Manual Command Argument Testing

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This Bash script outlines a series of manual tests for validating how a given command handles various types of arguments, including no arguments, single arguments, multiple arguments, and arguments with special characters like spaces. It expects the command to be tested as its first input.

```bash
COMMAND="$1"

echo "Testing argument handling for /$COMMAND"
echo

echo "Test 1: No arguments"
echo "  Command: /$COMMAND"
echo "  Expected: [describe expected behavior]"
echo "  Manual test required"
echo

echo "Test 2: Single argument"
echo "  Command: /$COMMAND test-value"
echo "  Expected: 'test-value' appears in output"
echo "  Manual test required"
echo

echo "Test 3: Multiple arguments"
echo "  Command: /$COMMAND arg1 arg2 arg3"
echo "  Expected: All arguments used appropriately"
echo "  Manual test required"
echo

echo "Test 4: Special characters"
echo "  Command: /$COMMAND \"value with spaces\""
echo "  Expected: Entire phrase captured"
echo "  Manual test required"

```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow for Claude Command Validation

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This YAML configuration defines a CI workflow for validating Claude commands. It runs on push and pull requests, checking command structure, frontmatter, and ensuring no TODOs exist in the command files, thereby maintaining code quality and consistency.

```yaml
name: Test Commands

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Validate command structure
        run: |
          for cmd in .claude/commands/*.md; do
            echo "Testing: $cmd"
            ./scripts/validate-command.sh "$cmd"
          done

      - name: Validate frontmatter
        run: |
          for cmd in .claude/commands/*.md; do
            ./scripts/validate-frontmatter.sh "$cmd"
          done

      - name: Check for TODOs
        run: |
          if grep -r "TODO" .claude/commands/; then
            echo "ERROR: TODOs found in commands"
            exit 1
          fi
```

### Essential Testing Tips for Claude Plugin Commands (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

This section provides essential development tips for effectively testing Claude plugin commands. It covers verifying functionality with the plugin installed, debugging environment variable expansion, and ensuring command resilience across different working directories and resource availability.

```Bash
cd /path/to/plugin
claude /command-name args
```

```Bash
# Add debug output to command
!`echo "Plugin root: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"`
```

```Bash
cd /tmp && claude /command-name
cd /other/project && claude /command-name
```

```Bash
# Check all plugin resources exist
!`ls -la ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/`
!`ls -la ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/`
```

### Examples of Effective and Ineffective Plugin Command Naming

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

This snippet provides examples of both good and bad plugin command naming conventions. Effective names are descriptive, action-oriented, use hyphens for multi-word commands, and consider plugin prefixes for uniqueness, while generic or ambiguous names should be avoided to prevent conflicts and ensure clarity.

```text
Good:
- /mylyn-sync          (plugin-specific prefix)
- /analyze-performance (descriptive action)
- /docker-compose-up   (clear purpose)

Avoid:
- /test               (conflicts with common name)
- /run                (too generic)
- /do-stuff           (not descriptive)
```

### Basic Slash Command File Format - Markdown with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/README.md

Demonstrates the fundamental structure of a Claude Code slash command file. Commands use Markdown format with optional YAML frontmatter for configuration. The frontmatter defines metadata like description, allowed tools, and argument hints, followed by the command prompt content that can include dynamic arguments, file references, and bash execution.

```markdown
---
description: Brief description
argument-hint: [arg1] [arg2]
allowed-tools: Read, Bash(git:*)
---

Command prompt content with:
- Arguments: $1, $2, or $ARGUMENTS
- Files: @path/to/file
- Bash: !`command here`
```

### Validate File References with @ Syntax - Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

Bash command to check for @ syntax usage in command markdown files. This grep command searches for @ symbols in the my-command.md file to verify proper file reference syntax in the .claude/commands directory.

```bash
grep '@' .claude/commands/my-command.md
```

### Manual-Only Command with disable-model-invocation

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configured for manual-only execution with disable-model-invocation set to true. Example demonstrates preventing automated invocation for commands requiring user judgment, such as production deployment approvals.

```markdown
---
description: Approve deployment to production
disable-model-invocation: true
---
```

### Specify Model for Claude Code Command Execution (YAML Frontmatter)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This YAML snippet illustrates how to specify the `model` to be used for executing a Claude Code command. Users can choose models like `haiku`, `sonnet`, or `opus` based on the command's complexity and performance requirements. This field overrides the conversation's default model for that specific command.

```yaml
---
model: haiku
---
```

### Configure Command Hook for Deterministic Checks (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/hook-development/SKILL.md

This configuration defines a command hook that executes a bash script for deterministic checks and operations. It sets the hook type to 'command', provides the bash command to run, and includes a timeout. Command hooks are suitable for fast validations, file system operations, and integrating external tools where performance is critical.

```json
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/validate.sh",
  "timeout": 60
}
```

### Validate Command Argument with Bash Regex

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

This pattern demonstrates a common approach to validate a command argument using a Bash `grep -E` regular expression. It checks if the input matches a defined pattern and returns 'OK' or 'ERROR' for conditional logic.

```Bash
!`echo "$1" | grep -E "^pattern$" && echo "OK" || echo "ERROR"`
```

### Organize Complex Commands with Commented Markdown Sections

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/documentation-patterns.md

This snippet demonstrates how to use commented markdown sections (e.g., `<!-- SECTION 1: VALIDATION -->`) within a command's implementation. This practice helps logically group different stages of a complex command, such as validation, analysis, and recommendations, improving readability and maintainability for developers.

```markdown
---
description: Complex multi-step command
---

<!-- SECTION 1: VALIDATION -->
<!-- This section checks prerequisites before proceeding -->

Checking prerequisites...
- Git repository: !`git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null`
- Branch exists: [validation logic]

<!-- SECTION 2: ANALYSIS -->
<!-- Analyzes the differences between branches -->

Analyzing differences between $1 and $2...
[Analysis logic...]

<!-- SECTION 3: RECOMMENDATIONS -->
<!-- Provides actionable recommendations -->

Based on analysis, recommend:
[Recommendations...]

<!-- END: Next steps for user -->
```

### Claude Command: Generate Documentation for a File

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/simple-commands.md

This command defines a 'document' action for Claude to generate comprehensive documentation for a given source file. It takes a file path as an argument and instructs Claude to include an overview, API documentation, usage examples, and implementation notes, formatted as Markdown.

```markdown
---
description: Generate documentation for file
argument-hint: [source-file]
---

Generate comprehensive documentation for @$1

Include:

**Overview:**
- Purpose and responsibility
- Main functionality
- Dependencies

**API Documentation:**
- Function/method signatures
- Parameter descriptions with types
- Return values with types
- Exceptions/errors thrown

**Usage Examples:**
- Basic usage
- Common patterns
- Edge cases

**Implementation Notes:**
- Algorithm complexity
- Performance considerations
- Known limitations

Format as Markdown suitable for project documentation.
```

```bash
> /document src/api/users.ts
```

### Validate Plugin Resources in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Illustrates how to check the availability of critical plugin resources before proceeding with an operation. This command verifies the existence of a config file, script directory, and an executable tool using multiple Bash `test` commands.

```markdown
---
description: Run analysis with plugin tools
allowed-tools: Bash(test:*)
---

Validate plugin setup:
- Config exists: !`test -f ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config.json && echo "✓" || echo "✗"`
- Scripts exist: !`test -d ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts && echo "✓" || echo "✗"`
- Tools available: !`test -x ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/analyze && echo "✓" || echo "✗"`

If all checks pass, proceed with analysis.
Otherwise, report missing components and installation steps.
```

--------------------------------

### Declare Required Tools with Allowed-Tools

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

Shows how to properly declare and grant permissions for tools used in plugin commands. Without declaring allowed-tools in the frontmatter, commands using Bash or other restricted tools will fail with permission errors.

```markdown
# Missing allowed-tools
!`bash script.sh`  # Will fail without Bash permission

# Correct
---
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---
!`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/script.sh`
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Allowed Tools for Claude Code Command (YAML Frontmatter)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This YAML snippet demonstrates how to configure the `allowed-tools` for a Claude Code command. This field controls which tools Claude can access during command execution, enhancing security and operational control. It can accept specific tool names (e.g., `Read, Write`), patterns (`Bash(git:*)`), or an array of tools.

```yaml
---
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash(git:*)
---
```

--------------------------------

### Filter Bash Commands by Pattern in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

These YAML snippets demonstrate how to restrict Bash tool access to specific command patterns using filters like `git:*`, `npm:*`, or `docker:*`. This granular control enhances security by preventing arbitrary Bash command execution, allowing only predefined operations related to specific tools. It ensures commands can only perform actions relevant to their purpose.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*)           # Only git commands
```

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(npm:*)           # Only npm commands
```

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(docker:*)        # Only docker commands
```
